title: Aircraft
abstract: An aircraft is provided that is convertible in flight between a rotary wing configuration and a fixed wing configuration. In its fixed wing configuration the aircraft resembles a Blended Wing Body (BWB) having a swept wing angle Î². Conversions from the fixed wing configuration to the rotary wing configuration, and vice versa, are accomplished by flipping an outboard portion of one wing through 180 to reorient the leading edge of the outboard portion by an angle of 2Î² to establish a reverse sweep. In its rotary configuration, the entire aircraft is rotated.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1 . An aircraft, wherein the aircraft is convertible in-flight between a rotary wing configuration R and a fixed wing configuration F, the aircraft comprising: a base member, wherein the base member defines a symmetrical plane; a pair of outboard wing portions extending from the base member on opposite sides of the symmetrical plane, wherein each outboard wing portion has a leading edge and the leading edge is oriented at an angle Î² relative' to the symmetrical plane to have the aircraft resemble a Blended Wing Body (BWB) in the fixed wing configuration F; an actuator mounted on the base member at a distance d from the symmetrical plane for flipping an outboard wing portion of the aircraft about a reconfiguration axis perpendicular to the symmetrical plane to convert the aircraft from F to R and vice versa, wherein the outboard wing portion is at a distance greater than d from the symmetrical plane; at least one thruster mounted on the aircraft at a distance s from the symmetrical plane, and wherein the thruster propels the entire aircraft in fixed wing flight when the aircraft is in F, and rotates the entire aircraft for rotary wing flight when the aircraft is in R; and a controller mounted on the base member and connected to the actuator for converting the aircraft between F and R, and connected to the thruster for controlling forces exerted on the base member of the aircraft. 2. An aircraft as recited in claim 1 wherein a pair of thrusters are each mounted on the aircraft on opposite sides of the symmetrical plane. 3. An aircraft as recited in claim 2 wherein s > d. 4. An aircraft as recited in claim 2 wherein the pair of thrusters include a first thruster mounted on the aircraft at a distance si from the symmetrical plane, and a second thruster mounted on the aircraft at a distance S2 from the symmetrical plane. 5. An aircraft as recited in claim 4 wherein si is not equal to S2. 6. An aircraft as recited in claim 1 wherein a wing span for F of the aircraft is in a range of 5 ft. to 150 ft. 7. An aircraft as recited in claim 1 wherein the aircraft has a sweep angle Î² in F, and Î² is in a range between 0Â° and 60Â°. 8. An aircraft as recited in claim 1 wherein the outboard wing portion is flipped 180Â° to change an orientation of its leading edge by an angle of 2Î². 9. An aircraft as recited in claim 2 wherein the thrusters are embedded in the aircraft. 10. A method for flying an aircraft which comprises the steps of: rotating the entire aircraft at an angular velocity Ï around an axis when the aircraft is configured in a rotary wing configuration R for rotary wing flight; propelling the aircraft at a forward airspeed V, when Ï = 0 and the aircraft is configured in a fixed wing configuration F; controlling the aircraft in rotary wing flight maneuvers with combined collective and cyclic controls when the aircraft is in R, and further controlling the aircraft in fixed wing flight maneuvers with conventional fixed wing controls when the aircraft is in F; reconfiguring the aircraft in flight between R and F; and initiating the reconfiguring step when the aircraft has established a linear momentum vector MV above a predetermined value, wherein the velocity V, of MV, has a predetermined magnitude with a detectable direction. 1 1. A method as recited in claim 10 wherein the aircraft has a base member defining a symmetrical plane with a pair of outboard wing portions extending from the base member on opposite sides of the symmetrical plane, wherein each outboard wing portion has a leading edge and the leading edge is oriented at an angle Î² relative to the symmetrical plane to have the aircraft resemble a Blended Wing Body (BWB) in the fixed wing configuration F, and further wherein the reconfiguring step is selected from the group of steps consisting of. flipping an outboard wing portion of one wing of the aircraft through an angle of 180Â° about a reconfiguration axis perpendicular to the symmetrical plane to change an orientation of its leading edge by a positive angle 2Î² to convert the aircraft from F to R; and flipping the outboard wing portion of one wing of the aircraft through an angle of 180Â° about the reconfiguration axis to change an orientation of its leading edge by a negative angle of 2Î² to convert the aircraft from R to F. 12. A method as recited in claim 1 1 wherein the fixed wing configuration F is characterized by a pair of thrusters mounted on the aircraft on opposite sides of the symmetrical plane from each other at a distance s from the symmetrical plane, and wherein both thrusters are used to propel the entire aircraft in fixed wing flight at an airspeed V, when the aircraft is in F. 13. A method as recited in claim 12 wherein the rotary wing configuration R is characterized by a flipped outboard wing portion and wherein the thrusters are used to rotate the entire aircraft at the angular velocity Ï for rotary wing flight. 14. A method as recited in claim 12 wherein the reconfiguring step is initiated before Ï in R and V in F cause the airfoils to stall. 15. An aircraft which comprises: means for rotating the entire aircraft at an angular velocity Ï around an axis when the aircraft is configured in a rotary wing configuration R for rotary wing flight; means for propelling the aircraft at a forward airspeed V, when Ï = 0 and the aircraft is configured in a fixed wing configuration F; means for controlling the aircraft in rotary wing flight maneuvers with combined collective and cyclic controls when the aircraft is in R, and further for controlling the aircraft in fixed wing flight maneuvers with conventional fixed wing controls when the aircraft is in F; and means for reconfiguring the aircraft in flight between R and F. 16. An aircraft as recited in claim 15 wherein the means for rotating the entire aircraft comprises a pair of thrusters, wherein each thruster is mounted on the aircraft on opposite sides of the symmetrical plane from each other at a distance s from the symmetrical plane, and wherein the thrusters are operationally oriented to create a force couple on the aircraft to rotate the entire aircraft at the angular velocity Ï for rotary wing flight when the aircraft is in R. 17. An aircraft as recited in claim 15 wherein the means for propelling the aircraft comprises a pair of thrusters, wherein each thruster is mounted on the aircraft on opposite sides of the symmetrical plane from each other at a distance s from the symmetrical plane, and wherein the thrusters propel the aircraft in fixed wing flight with a flight velocity V when the aircraft is in F. 18. An aircraft as recited in claim 15 wherein the aircraft has a base member defining a symmetrical plane, and the means for reconfiguring the aircraft in flight between R and F comprises: a pair of outboard wing portions extending from the base member on opposite sides of the symmetrical plane, wherein each outboard wing portion has a leading edge and the leading edge is oriented at an angle Î² relative to the symmetrical plane to have the aircraft resemble a Blended Wing Body (BWB) in the fixed wing configuration F; and an actuator mounted on the base member at a distance d from the symmetrical plane for flipping an outboard wing portion of the aircraft about a reconfiguration axis perpendicular to the symmetrical plane to convert the aircraft from F to R and vice versa, wherein the outboard wing portion is at a distance greater than d from the symmetrical plane. 19. An aircraft as recited in claim 18 wherein the outboard wing portion is flipped 180Â° to change an orientation of its leading edge by an angle of 2Î². 20. An aircraft as recited in claim 15 wherein a wing span for F of the aircraft is in a range of 5 ft. to 150 ft., and the aircraft has a sweep angle Î² in F, in a range between 0Â° and 60Â°.
